title: PLA De-Escalates to 2 Sorties on April 3 — Oscillating Pattern Since March 29 Peak
detail: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 2 PLA aircraft sorties around Taiwan on April 3, 2026 — a sharp decrease from 11 sorties recorded on April 1 — continuing the oscillating post-peak pattern that has characterized PLA signaling since the March 29 high of 19 sorties. Both sorties entered Taiwan's southwestern and eastern ADIZ; 7 PLAN vessels and 2 official ships were also operating around Taiwan. Taiwan's Armed Forces deployed CAP aircraft, Navy ships, and coastal missile systems in response. The drop from 11 (April 1) to 2 (April 3) is consistent with PLA 'spike-and-retract' signaling observed throughout the late-March escalation and is likely designed to maintain ambiguity ahead of KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's April 7-12 China visit — now 4 days away — allowing Beijing to signal both coercive pressure and diplomatic openness simultaneously.

title: PLA Warship Warns US Navy P-8 Near Hengchun Peninsula — Claims 24nm Zone Violation
detail: At 6:47 a.m. local time on April 3, 2026, a US Navy P-8 Poseidon maritime patrol aircraft conducting surveillance near Taiwan's Hengchun Peninsula in Pingtung County received a warning from a PLA Navy warship claiming the aircraft had entered China's 24-nautical-mile contiguous zone. The US Navy maintains its legal right to operate in international airspace throughout the Western Pacific and has not acknowledged any restriction in the area. Such PLA warship warnings to US ISR aircraft are a recognized escalatory tactic designed to pressure US surveillance operations near Taiwan and normalize PRC maritime domain assertions. The incident coincides with reduced PLA air activity on April 3 (2 sorties vs. 11 on April 1), suggesting Beijing is operating on multiple coercive vectors simultaneously ahead of the KMT chair's April 7-12 China visit.

title: KMT China Visit Itinerary Released: Shanghai → Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum (Apr 8) → Beijing; Xi Meeting Confirmed
detail: With 4 days before KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's departure, the full itinerary of her April 7-12 China visit was confirmed: the delegation will travel Shanghai → Nanjing (Jiangsu Province) → Shanghai → Beijing. The most symbolically charged stop is the Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum in Nanjing, scheduled for April 8 — a site Beijing uses to position KMT as custodians of a shared Chinese national heritage within a unification narrative. Cheng will meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing. Beijing's selection of the Sun Yat-sen mausoleum is calculated: the KMT was founded by Sun Yat-sen, and China frames the mausoleum visit as KMT honoring its 'Chinese roots' — a symbolism incompatible with Taiwan independence and useful for presenting cross-strait relations as naturally trending toward unification. Taiwan national security officials reiterated concern that the visit is designed to create an 'illusion of unification momentum' for Trump ahead of the May 14-15 summit. DPP legislators called on KMT members not to 'carry out Beijing's agenda' during ongoing defense budget negotiations, where the KMT caucus has proposed NT$380B against the DPP's NT$1.25T bill.
